name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-57
title: scope-guard.py path-prefix comparisons use string startswith on REPO_ROOT path — symlink/realpath edge case
description: hooks/scope-guard.py lines 107, 123-125, 149, 162, 163: all path comparisons use str.startswith(str(REPO_ROOT) + '/'). REPO_ROOT is derived from 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' which returns the symlink-resolved real path. But abs_path is built from os.getcwd() or the raw tool_input (line 60-61) which preserves any symlink components. If REPO_ROOT is, say, /home/user/proj but the user invokes Claude in /tmp/proj (a symlink to /home/user/proj), every check returns 'outside repo' (line 108) and writes are silently allowed everywhere. The os.path.realpath call at line 100 only handles the case where the TARGET is a symlink, not where cwd is symlinked. Resolve abspath via os.path.realpath(abspath(target)) defensively, or compare against both realpath and lexical-path of REPO_ROOT.

history.note: wave-4 cleanup; fixed in e10610a (impl) and 91f2c68 (tests)
